text: ⚡️ 🇪🇺 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 The Russian Ministry of Defence has released a list of companies that produce drones for Ukraine under joint production agreements signed between Ukraine and several European countries on March 26th. Moscow described the move as a deliberate escalation that could worsen the military-political situation across Europe and turn European countries into a strategic rear base for Ukraine. They warned that potential drone strikes on Russian territory using systems produced in Europe could lead to unpredictable consequences. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to OSINT OSINTdefender RT @HM_Int3lligence : Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov this week, describing the relationship between China and Russia as "precious" and calling for deeper cooperation between the two countries. During the meeting, Lavrov offered to increase Russia's energy supplies to China to help offset disruptions caused by the Iran war, including shipping constraints through the Strait of Hormuz. Russian President Vladimir Putin is also expected to visit China soon. China has positioned itself as a stabilizing force amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, with several world leaders meeting Xi this week, including Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Abu Dhabi's Crown Prince Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and Vietnamese leader To Lam. Gulf countries are reportedly hoping China will use its influence over Iran to reduce attacks on neighboring countries and bring Tehran to the negotiation table. Dylan Loh, an associate professor at Singapore's Nanyang Technological University, noted that China holds leverage over Iran and there are growing expectations that it will use that influence more directly. Source: AFP, AP, Reuters So What The Iran situation is likely highly beneficial for Russia, particularly in terms of energy sales to India and China. While China has attempted to position itself as a stabilizing force in the region, it is highly unlikely to deploy military assets in numbers large enough to deter the United States. A more probable course of action is that China will continue to supply Iran with weapons and equipment, with the intention of drawing the US further into another Middle East conflict. Regardless of China's approach, this is unlikely to have a significant impact on American decision-making. Follow us to join the intelligence community! #HMIntelligence #HM #Iran #US #China tweet

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 NBC: The U.S. didn't ask for a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah during negotiations on Tuesday, but a U.S. official did empahses that the U.S. wants to see a durable peace between the two. “This is not something we have asked for, nor is it part of the peace negotiations with Iran, but the president would welcome the end of hostilities in Lebanon as part of a peace agreement between Israel and Lebanon. The United States’ focus is on building trust between the two governments so that we can create space for a peace deal and so that any future understandings can be durable. Both sides need to build political momentum.” @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷Mehr: Iran has suspended petrochemical exports for a period in order to stabilize the domestic market. They specified that this doesn't include crude oil. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Admin note: This comes as Israeli airstrikes targeted key producers of these goods with significant damage to major facilities in the South Pars region, including Mahshahr, Bandar Imam, and Asaluyeh. Petrochemicals constitute nearly 33% of Iran's total non-oil exports and are the second-largest source of revenue after crude oil. Roughly 85% of Iran's petrochemical export capacity was disabled due to the aforementioned strikes on major hubs, leaving only 10%–15% operational. Previously, Iran boasted a nominal production capacity near 95 million tons per year. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to OSINT Status-6 (War & Military News) It is believed that the notorious pro-Kremlin Russian milblogger and crew-member of Kamov Ka-52 attack helicopter, Alexey Zemtsov, known on Telegram as "Voevoda Broadcasts (Воевода Вещает)," has committed suicide. In a lengthy, 16-min video published on Telegram on Wednesday Zemtsov describes his past conflicts with superiors over his online activity. But the main plot is this: in October 2025, "Voevoda" becomes aware that his wife is cheating on him, so he decides to beat the guy whom she is sleeping with. For that he is taken to custody and later sent to assault units. During courses for platoon commanders in April 2026, "Voevoda" publishes a photo of an old, Soviet-made SSh-68 steel helmet that was distributed to a serviceman who was supposed to take part in combat operations. For that, "Voevoda" is accused of discreditation of the Russian army and threatened with return to custody. "I don't want to live with the shame, with discreditation of the Russian army," milblogger Alexey Zemtsov declared in his farewell video. tweet

OSINTtechnical A blaze tore through one of Australia's two remaining oil refineries overnight. The Corio Refinery produces 50% of Victoria's and 10% of Australia's fuel, with Australia’s energy minister adding that jet fuel and gasoline production would be disrupted. https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2044534830747009254/photo/1 tweet

However, the President would welcome and be happy with an end of hostilities as part of an agreement between…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 Axios: The possibility of a ceasefire was discussed in a meeting between U.S. Secretary of State Rubio, Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Leiter, and Lebanese Ambassador to the U.S. Hamadeh. A source says the proposal included confidence-building measures by both sides, with Israel expected to consider a ceasefire as part of the framework. The Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors said they would consult with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun before returning with responses. @wfwitness

Status-6 (War & Military News) RT @DanLamothe : Attempted evasion reported: Yesterday, an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel tried to evade the U.S. blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas, exiting the Strait of Hormuz, and transiting along the Iranian coastline. The guided-missile destroyer USS Spruance (DDG 111) successfully redirected the vessel, which is heading back to Iran. Ten vessels have now been turned around and ZERO ships have broken through since the start of the U.S. blockade on Monday. - U.S. Central Command tweet
